factors: I made the assumption that the wizard always had the ability to place a sign, and also KNEW where the gold was at all times. This means that if the wizard knows where the gold is and tells the farmer, he is allowing the farmer to get it. If the wizard tells him where the gold is and the farmer chooses to NOT listen, then either direction would not have mattered, so it made no difference. With the rocks, if the wizard knows where the gold is, and intentionally leads the farmer to the rocks, then that is MAKING him do it. This assumes the farmer in that specific instance will always choose to listen. In the instances of not listening, and the farmer chooses the rocks, then it also means the wizard made no difference. Allowing has a positive connotation, enabling a negative connotation.
